references.text: Meijden, R van der & N Caspers. 1971. Haloragaceae. Flora Malesiana series 1, 7: 239-259 (page reference excludes Gunnera [now in Gunneraceae]

species.citation: (Lour.) Orchard , Bull. Auckland Inst. Mus. 10 (1975) 207; ≡ Haloragis chinensis (Lour.) Merr. , Trans. New York Acad. Sci., N.S. 24 (1935) 290 ; Meijden & Caspers, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 7 (1971) 243. = Haloragis scabra var. elongata Schindl. , Pflanzenr. (Engler) IV, 225 (1905) 29 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 221.
species.distribution: Australia, Borneo, China, Hong Kong, Lesser Sunda Isls, Moluccas, New Guinea, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Singapore, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Vietnam. BUSUANGA, CALAMIANES, CULION, LUZON: Benguet, Ilocos Sur, MINDANAO: Lanao del Sur.

species.citation: (Merr.) Orchard , Bull. Auckland Inst. Mus. 10 (1975) 187; ≡ Haloragis halconensis Merr. , PJS 2c (1907) 288 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 221; Meijden & Caspers, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 7 (1971) 245.
species.distribution: New Guinea, Philippines, Solomon Isls, Sulawesi. LUZON: Quezon, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Cotabato, Davao, MINDORO, NEGROS.
species.notes: Open heaths in exposed ridges, cliffs, streamlets, etc. Fl. & fr. Jan-Oct. 900-3800m.

species.citation: Thunb. , Nov. Gen. Pl. (Thunberg) (1783) 55; ≡ Haloragis micrantha (Thunb.) R.Br. ex Siebold & Zucc. , Fl. Jap. Fam. Nat. 1 (1845) 25 ; R.Br. in Flinders, Voy. App. (1814) 550, nom. inval.; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 221; Meijden & Caspers, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 7 (1971) 244.
species.distribution: Australia, Borneo, China, India, Japan, Java, Korea, New Guinea, New Zealand, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam. LUZON: Benguet, Ifugao, Nueva Vizcaya, MINDANAO: Cotabato, Davao, Davao del Sur, MINDORO, NEGROS.
species.notes: Bogs in high-montane meadows, streamlets, ponds and wet ridges. Fl. throughout the year. (1000-)1600-3600m.

species.citation: (Merr.) Orchard , Bull. Auckland Inst. Mus. 10 (1975) 211; ≡ Haloragis philippinensis Merr. , PJS 1(Suppl.) (1906) 216 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 221; Meijden & Caspers, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 7 (1971) 242.
species.distribution: India, Myanmar, Philippines, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. LUZON: Benguet, Ilocos Sur, Mountain Province, MINDANAO: Cotabato.
species.notes: Open grassy slopes and on banks in pine forests. 1200-3400m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 16 January 2022

species.citation: (Vell.) Verdc. , Kew Bull. 28 (1973) 36; ≡ Enydria aquatica Vell. , Fl. Flumin. (1829) 57. = Myriophyllum brasiliense Cambess. , Fl. Bras. Merid. (A. St.-Hil.) 2 (1830) 252 ; Meijden & Caspers, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 7 (1971) 253.
species.notes: J.V. LaFrankie 10-Jul-13: "Probably naturalized in various parts of the country". 400-1500m.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 992 ; Merrill, PJS 1(Suppl.) (1906) 217; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 221; Meijden & Caspers, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 7 (1971) 253.
species.distribution: Philippines, Sumatra. MINDANAO: Lanao del Sur.
species.notes: Throughout the N hemisphere, rare in the tropics; record in the Philippines: Lake Lanao, Clemens 450, cited by Merrill 1923; shallow lake water. 670-900m.
